title: Evangelion 222 Ending Credits Japan vs USA
display_title: Evangelion 222 Ending Credits Japan vs USA
wikitext:
[]: Evangelion 2.22 Ending Credits [Japan vs. USA]             Quote Post by squall.leonhart » Fri May 06, 2011 6:53 pm [s:2dr8e9p7]http://youtu.be/Rlgi1Cw7zQE?hd=1[/s:2dr8e9p7] http://3xr-ani.me/video/Evangelion_2.22_Ending_Credits_[Japan_vs._USA].mp4 I think the video speaks for itself here. EDIT: F**KYOU YOUTUBE! I'm uploading it to my site. I'll have a link in a bit.

[]:             Quote Post by Shogo-Kun » Fri May 06, 2011 8:15 pm Wait... You're not... You're really not.... Are we really gonna have a thread about this? Are we really gonna do this?

[]:             Quote Post by Tarnsman » Fri May 06, 2011 8:29 pm Things that I'm very minorly annoyed by: The English credits scroll too slowly, Dub cast credited as "Cast" and shown first with the Japanese cast being credited as "Japanese Cast" and not shown until like half way through it. Things I really don't care about: The stupid pointless rainbow effect getting removed.

[]:             Quote Post by Legendary » Fri May 06, 2011 8:58 pm Things I'm very much annoyed by: A post consisting of nothing more than a link and a disclaimer that there's nothing else to say. Come on, man. Summarize it for those of us who are currently unable to watch any cool vidyas on YouTube.

[]:             Quote Post by Clover » Fri May 06, 2011 9:06 pm Tarnsman wrote: Things I really don't care about: The stupid pointless rainbow effect getting removed.

[]:             Quote Post by Shogo-Kun » Fri May 06, 2011 9:09 pm Ya know what? I was gonna stay silent about this, but I have so say it because this just bugs the hell out me. Things I'm annoyed by: a topic dedicated to something VERY FEW PEOPLE CARE ABOUT!!! It's the freakin' credits! Why are we talking about something that has no affect on the ENJOYMENT of Rebuild?! I know it sounds like I'm ranting, but the existence of this just irritates and fascinates me! I mean... *shrug* Wow.... Just... wow.

[]:             Quote Post by squall.leonhart » Fri May 06, 2011 11:39 pm I'm not hating on anybody. It just shows how little Funimation actually cared to credit the people that actually made the movie. Instead, they only credit themselves for the first half and then the key people involved for the second half. They don't even list the title of the song that's playing. I realize that most people will just blow this off and not care, but it's has to deal with respect for the individual. I don't care if you think I'm stupid or petty that I'd nitpick something as low as the credits, but if I had helped create this, I'd want my name recognized by someone. It's also why filmmakers put additional scenes after the credits; they want you to stay. Whether or not you can read the names or not, you should still respect them. In regards to the YouTube link, I removed the video because it was getting blocked due to the song. So I just deleted it and am now hosting it on my website. The file is 44MB and is double-wide SD.

[]:             Quote Post by Legendary » Fri May 06, 2011 11:50 pm My complaint is more "Not everyone can look at videos at any given moment." Sometimes you're just skimming the internet really quickly and need an actual complaint, like what you just put there to summarize the problem.

[]:             Quote Post by Tarnsman » Sat May 07, 2011 12:17 am squall.leonhart wrote: I'm not hating on anybody. It just shows how little Funimation actually cared to credit the people that actually made the movie. Instead, they only credit themselves for the first half and then the key people involved for the second half. They don't even list the title of the song that's playing. I realize that most people will just blow this off and not care, but it's has to deal with respect for the individual. I don't care if you think I'm stupid or petty that I'd nitpick something as low as the credits, but if I had helped create this, I'd want my name recognized by someone. It's also why filmmakers put additional scenes after the credits; they want you to stay. Whether or not you can read the names or not, you should still respect them. In regards to the YouTube link, I removed the video because it was getting blocked due to the song. So I just deleted it and am now hosting it on my website. The file is 44MB and is double-wide SD.

[]:             Quote Post by Warren Peace » Sat May 07, 2011 12:35 am Yeah, when is Obama gonna send the seals after the people responsible for THIS!?

[]:             Quote Post by EvangelionFan » Sat May 07, 2011 2:13 am On serious note, Funimation did something similar with the Summer Wars BD - I noticed it after watching it in Japanese with subs, so I can only assume they opted not to include the original Japanese credit reel at all. So... I guess you're better off talking about something like this on the Funimation forums, as users/mods there might be able to tell you why it was done.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Sat May 07, 2011 6:12 am Well, it would make sense to put the names of ther english voice actors in there/ if you watched the dub, you technically didn't hear any work by the original voice actors at all. The fact that they're mentioned at all is in itself a gesture of respect. But... The Rainbow effect T~T You can't have Rebuild without evangelion, that's like EoE without boobies!

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Sat May 07, 2011 7:23 pm To be honest, I don't care. The rainbow was cool and all, and I'm glad I saw it when it hit theaters, but it's not something I'm gonna fight for. But, seriously guys. PIXAR credits are worth talking about, not NME credits.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sat May 07, 2011 10:21 pm My thoughts are with Tarnsman's. As far as the rainbow credit reels--while I can understand that they weren't included after each film, they should have been included as an Extra or something. At the very least, I hope whenever Renewal Death/EoE would be released here, the Japanese Death and EoE credits (Sunset and DNA Helix credits) are included after each film or as an extra because those ARE works of art.

[]:             Quote Post by Hatsumi92 » Sat May 07, 2011 10:54 pm I'm pretty sure Funi's credits credited most major people involved. Sometimes credits are supplied by the Japanese license holder and they have to get approval from the Japanese license holder too so this is apparently fine. As much as I'd of liked to see it go Japanese cast then English cast because the Japanese cast are the ones which created the show not the English cast but that's my personal opinion. Nothing against the dub, I love the dub it's great.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva 04 » Sat May 07, 2011 11:47 pm Does it really matter? They're just credits. It's a minor thing and honestly isnt worth talking about since Funi does this with a lot of anime they licensed.

[]:             Quote Post by Legendary » Sun May 08, 2011 1:03 am Plus Kendrix has a point. The English voice cast SHOULD be first, as they're the ones that an English dub features. This isn't like live-action; there are NO traces of a voice actor once you start dubbing over it. The Japanese voice cast being on at all is actually pretty decent! I could see other companies finding ways to not bother with that at all!

[]:             Quote Post by Dr. Nick » Sun May 08, 2011 3:09 am Ruining ending credits seems to be an endemic problem with Funimation. It's kind of bizarre how this giant licensing company completely freezes up whenever they encounter an ending sequence more complex than rolling text on a plain black background. What year is this, 1997? As a translator, I find this infuriating because the solution is such a blatantly obvious one. Let the Japanese credits roll, and then add a separate English-language credit roll after them. It's a cheap, fast and effective method, and a lot of companies have used it in the past. I'd urge Funi's American customers to send them constructive criticism regarding this terrible practice. That Mania thread seems to imply they do actually listen to legit complaints.

[]:             Quote Post by Shogo-Kun » Sun May 08, 2011 8:41 am @Dr. Nick: You mean the same double credit treatment ADV, Mangle, and Bandai gives to some of their anime film releases? UGH!!! No thanks! I'd rather have standard rolling credits over that anyday. Thay treatment just feels really ugly and tacky to me.

[]:             Quote Post by Dr. Nick » Sun May 08, 2011 9:39 am Shogo-Kun wrote: @Dr. Nick: You mean the same double credit treatment ADV, Mangle, and Bandai gives to some of their anime film releases? UGH!!! No thanks! I'd rather have standard rolling credits over that anyday. Thay treatment just feels really ugly and tacky to me.
